channel.title: NYT > World News
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Live Updates: Cease-Fire Unraveling as U.S. and Iran Trade Strikes Again
item.description: The U.S. military said that it had hit dozens of Iranian targets in its latest salvo. Iran’s army said that it had fired more drones at American military sites in the region, as the two sides tussle over the Strait of Hormuz.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

item.title: Residents of Spanish Towns Caught in Fierce Blazes Recount Harrowing Escapes
item.description: Many say communication was chaotic and even nonexistent as flames tore through the area, taking 12 lives in one of the deadliest wildfires on record in Spain.
item.dc:creator: Jason Horowitz, Carlos Barragán and Lynsey Chutel

item.title: Hard-Liners in Iran Want to Keep Fighting America
item.description: With much of their leadership killed in the war, Iran’s conservatives have sought to fill the void and intensify the fight against the United States.
item.dc:creator: Neil MacFarquhar
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Emile Ducke for The New York Times
item.media:description: Mourners in Mashhad, Iran, during Ayatollah Ali Khamenei’s funeral, on Thursday.

item.title: How Trump Failed to Secure the Strait of Hormuz in His Iran Deal
item.description: President Trump signed an agreement that Iran said gave it control of the waterway — and global energy supplies. Now, Iran’s military is violently asserting authority.
item.dc:creator: Edward Wong, Michael Crowley and Eric Schmitt
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: President Trump at Joint Base Andrews on Thursday. He recently said the memorandum of understanding reached with Iran was “over.”

item.title: Zelensky Announces Leadership Shuffle as War Turns in Ukraine’s Favor
item.description: President Volodymyr Zelensky said Ukraine’s prime minister, Yuliia Svyrydenko, would step down amid a broader shake-up in Ukraine’s senior leadership.
item.dc:creator: Andrew E. Kramer
item.media:credit: Brendan Hoffman for The New York Times
item.media:description: Yuliia Svyrydenko, prime minister of Ukraine, at her office in Kyiv, Ukraine, in September.

item.title: Lindsey Graham Championed Israel, Even as Wars Eroded American Support
item.description: The Republican senator from South Carolina had an intense interest in the Middle East, and was a staunch supporter of U.S. strikes on Iran.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Boxerman
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
War Crimes, Genocide and Crimes Against Humanity
item.media:description: Senator Lindsey Graham, in 2024 in Washington, leading a Republican news conference to condemn the Biden administration’s decision to pause the delivery of bombs to Israel.

item.title: Sheikh Hamad bin Khalifa, Ruler Who Transformed Qatar, Dies at 74
item.description: Under Sheikh Hamad, the tiny nation of Qatar developed immense natural gas wealth and cultivated influence far beyond its borders.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Nereim
item.media:description: Sheikh Hamad bin Khalifa Al Thani is often described as the founding father of modern Qatar.

item.title: How Putin Turned Japan Into a Den of Spies
item.description: Operating out of a Tokyo high-rise, a military intelligence unit finds the high-tech equipment that Russia needs to wage war.
item.dc:creator: Jane Bradley, Michael Schwirtz and Adam Goldman
item.media:credit: Chang W. Lee/The New York Times
item.media:description: The Aeroflot office in Toranomon Kotohira Tower in Tokyo. Japan’s weak espionage laws and flourishing high-tech industry have made it a crucial base for the Russian war effort.

item.title: At World Cup Ticket Prices, Even Messi Can’t Fill a Stadium
item.description: The Argentine team and its superstar, Lionel Messi, have been among the biggest draws at the World Cup, but that was not enough to prevent vacant seats at its quarterfinal on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Tariq Panja
item.media:description: Lionel Messi of Argentina on the field Saturday in Kansas City during a World Cup quarterfinal match against Switzerland.

item.title: As India Erases Its Colonial Past, Delhi’s Elite Feel Targeted
item.description: India’s government wants to shut down Delhi’s Gymkhana Club, which the prime minister calls a vestige of colonialism. Some members suspect a different motive.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy W. Peters, Pragati K.B. and Saumya Khandelwal
item.media:description: The Delhi Gymkhana Club is one of the oldest in India.

item.title: Inside Herat, Where a Taliban Campaign Targets a Cosmopolitan Outpost
item.description: Afghanistan’s leader has exerted full control over a city that once enjoyed looser social norms, even under Taliban rule.
item.dc:creator: Elian Peltier
item.media:credit: Jim Huylebroek for The New York Times
item.media:description: Women at a street market in Herat, Afghanistan, in 2023.

item.title: Typhoon Bavi Hits China, Causing Evacuations, Floods and Flight Delays
item.description: Typhoon Bavi made landfall in China after killing 17 in the Philippines, skirting Japan and hitting Taiwan. At its largest, the storm was about the width of France.
item.dc:creator: Lily Kuo and Pei-Lin Wu
item.media:description: The coastal village of Dongsha, in the Zhejiang province of China, was hit by Typhoon Bavi on Sunday.

item.title: U.K. Police Make New Arrest in Killing of Ex-Lawmaker
item.description: A 28-year-old man was detained on Saturday in connection with the death of Ann Widdecombe, a former Parliament member, the police said. Another man arrested on Friday was later released.
item.dc:creator: Michael D. Shear
item.media:description: Investigators on Saturday at the home of Ann Widdecombe in Devon, southwest England. Ms. Widdecombe, a longtime Conservative member of Parliament, was found dead there on Thursday.

item.title: England and Norway Resurrect a Rivalry for the World Cup
item.description: Along with historic and diplomatic ties, England’s club soccer teams have loyal fans in Norway. On Saturday, supporters will have to pick a side.
item.dc:creator: Lynsey Chutel
item.media:description: Jude Bellingham scoring his second goal of the match to give England an extra-time lead against Norway during Saturday’s quarterfinal.

item.title: Japan Is Building a New Intelligence Agency With Help From the West
item.description: Facing threats from Russia and China, Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi is breaking with World War II-era limits on security.
item.dc:creator: Javier C. Hernández
item.media:description: Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi at the Group of 7 summit in France last month. Ms. Takaichi wants Japan to do more to protect state secrets and vital technologies and more aggressively guard against foreign influence operations.

item.title: It’s the Scent of Manure to Most, but ‘the Smell of Money’ to Them
item.description: The Netherlands has been trying to figure out what to do with manure it can no longer use thanks to environmental rules. Volatile fertilizer costs may help.
item.dc:creator: Jeanna Smialek
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Maciek Nabrdalik for The New York Times

item.title: Why Young People in China Are Buying Feelings
item.description: Young people in China are increasingly anxious about the future and spending on feel-good experiences and products that provide “emotional value.” A companion hiker and cosplayer show what it’s like to be part of this booming consumer market.
item.dc:creator: Jiawei Wang, Jon Hazell and Rafaela Balster

item.title: Fire in Bangkok Bar Leaves at Least 27 Dead
item.description: The fast-moving blaze sent people fleeing toward the bathrooms to hide, officials said. Their bodies were found there, raising questions about safety standards.
item.dc:creator: Kittiphum Sringammuang, Ephrat Livni and Muktita Suhartono
item.media:description: At least 27 people died after the fire at Rong Beer Na Lat Phrao in the Thai capital, officials said.

item.title: With Lindsey Graham Gone, Ukraine Loses an Ally in Trump’s Ear
item.description: The South Carolina Republican was Ukraine’s most influential champion inside President Trump’s mostly ”America First” political orbit.
item.dc:creator: Robert Jimison
item.media:description: Senator Graham during a news conference after a meeting with President Volodymyr Zelensky of Ukraine in Kyiv on Friday.

item.title: Venezuela’s Post-Quake Politics
item.description: People are openly and forcefully voicing their anger at the government for the first time in years.
item.dc:creator: Katrin Bennhold
item.media:credit: Adriana Loureiro Fernandez for The New York Times
item.media:description: A collapsed public housing complex in Caraballeda, Venezuela.

item.title: Here’s a look at the heavy strikes in recent days.
item.description: The attack
item.dc:creator: Yan Zhuang and Aaron Boxerman
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: Vessels in the Strait of Hormuz, as seen from Musandam Peninsula, Oman, on Sunday. Oman said drones had been fired at sites in the area on Sunday.

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Eric Schmitt, Aaron Boxerman, Erica L. Green, Sanam Mahoozi and Hari Raj

item.title: Trump Sought an Iran War Exit. Putin Pushed On in Ukraine. Now Both Are Stuck.
item.description: The Iran and Ukraine wars underline the common limits of military force in achieving political ends, but also the differences between a dug-in Russian president and a vacillating American one.
item.dc:creator: Anton Troianovski and Paul Sonne
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: President Trump and President Vladimir V. Putin of Russia both appear convinced that their own war is just while the other’s is wrong.

item.title: Oil Prices Set for More Volatility After Latest Iranian Attack in Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Crude oil prices jumped above prewar levels this week, demonstrating Iran’s capacity to move energy prices by projecting power in the Strait of Hormuz.
item.dc:creator: Jason Karaian
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Boats anchored off Oman’s northern Musandam Peninsula near the Strait of Hormuz in June.

item.title: Toronto Police Search For Active Shooter After 2 Killed at Street Festival
item.description: The shooting disrupted a weekend Latino celebration where hundreds had gathered on Saturday evening.
item.dc:creator: Vjosa Isai
item.media:description: Police officers stand guard as emergency workers respond at the site of a deadly shooting at a salsa-themed street festival in Toronto on Saturday.

item.title: Here’s a look at the heavy strikes in recent days.
item.dc:creator: Zane Irwin
item.media:description: A vessel is seen in the Persian Gulf off the coast of Ajman, United Arab Emirates, on Friday.

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Pranav Baskar, Shirin Hakim, Aaron Boxerman and Yan Zhuang

item.title: Videos Shed Light on Pursuit Before ICE Killing in Houston
item.description: Surveillance footage obtained by The Times shows ICE agents driving aggressively in unmarked vehicles, but the moment of the fatal shooting of Lorenzo Salgado Araujo remains murky.
item.dc:creator: Robin Stein, Devon Lum, Sam Lerma, Mimi Dwyer, Alexander Cardia, Aric Toler, Dmitriy Khavin, Charlie Smart and Allison McCann

item.title: A Wildfire Killed 12 People in Southern Spain. Here’s the Latest.
item.description: Officials said more than 20 people were still missing because of the fire, one of the deadliest in Spain’s history. Most of those confirmed to have been killed were from Belgium or Britain.
item.dc:creator: Carlos Barragán and Lynsey Chutel
item.media:description: A wildfire moving toward residential buildings in El Pocico, Spain, on Friday.

item.title: 15 Killed as Speedboat Capsizes in Vietnam
item.description: A speedboat was carrying 36 people when it overturned near the island of Phu Quoc in southern Vietnam. The other people onboard were rescued alive.
item.dc:creator: Isabella Kwai and Tung Ngo
item.media:description: Rescue operations after a tourist vessel capsized off Phu Quoc island in southern Vietnam on Saturday.

item.title: U.S. Strikes Iran After Iran Fires on Ship in Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Iran’s attack on a container ship came hours after a meeting between the foreign ministers of Iran and Oman about opening the strait to traffic.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Boxerman and Pranav Baskar
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: The Strait of Hormuz seen from Musandam, Oman, this month. While the Trump administration has said that a cease-fire would fully lift Iran’s blockade of the strait, that has yet to be borne out.

item.title: Gravitational Pull
item.description: We think and dream and fuss and project entire worlds into existence. Meanwhile, the earth’s orbit continues and the sun goes on shining.
item.dc:creator: Melissa Kirsch

item.title: Inside the Canadian Lab Burning Down Buildings to Save Them
item.description: At the national fire safety facility in Ottawa, scientists and engineers are increasingly focused on the threat from the forest.
item.dc:creator: Ian Austen
item.media:description: Ahmed Kashef, the director of the National Research Council of Canada’s fire safety lab in Ottawa, inside a furnace used to test how long walls can withstand fire.

item.title: Hot Weather Sets Off Scramble for Outdoor Swimming in London
item.description: In an ever hotter city, the competition for pools is fierce. Some swimmers are turning to ponds and canals, which come with their own problems.
item.dc:creator: Isabella Kwai and Jane Stockdale
item.media:credit: Jane Stockdale for The New York Times

item.title: Israel Struck an Iranian Steel Facility. Was It a Valid Military Target?
item.description: During the war, Israel attacked Iran’s steel plants, saying they provided forces with revenue and the means to make weapons, but it also hurt the civilian economy.
item.dc:creator: Yeganeh Torbati
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Man Arrested in Killing of U.K. Ex-Lawmaker Is Released
item.description: The police said the 26-year-old was no longer part of the investigation into the death of Ann Widdecombe and urged anyone with information about the crime to come forward.
item.dc:creator: Michael D. Shear
item.media:description: Ms. Widdecombe in 2019. She retired as a Conservative Party lawmaker in 2010 and later joined the anti-immigration party Reform U.K.

item.title: U.S. Citizen Tests Positive for Ebola in Democratic Republic of Congo
item.description: The American, who works for the humanitarian organization Samaritan’s Purse, is the second to be infected in an outbreak that continues to spread.
item.dc:creator: Declan Walsh and John Yoon
item.category: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention
item.media:credit: Arlette Bashizi for The New York Times
item.media:description: An Ebola outbreak has killed hundreds of people in the Democratic Republic of Congo this year.

item.title: ‘They Don’t Need People’: The Workers Left Behind by China’s Robot Drive
item.description: For out-of-work factory workers in Kunshan, a region made rich by electronics manufacturing, a park is the only place to go.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Wang
item.media:description: Men looking for daily gigs in Kunshan, the heart of China’s manufacturing industry. As the country pivots toward automation, lower-skilled workers are finding fewer steady opportunities.

item.title: After Being Delayed by Trump, a Canada-U.S. Bridge Is Set to Open
item.description: A deal ended the Trump administration’s blocking of a new bridge that will ease congestion at the busiest trade corridor between the United States and Canada.
item.dc:creator: Ian Austen and Tyler Pager
item.category: Gordie Howe International Bridge (Detroit, Mich and Windsor, Ontario)
item.media:description: The Gordie Howe International Bridge connecting Detroit and Windsor, Ontario, in February.

item.title: Ten Killed in Plane Crash on the Bahamas’ Largest Island
item.description: The crash occurred as the country celebrated its independence from Britain.
item.dc:creator: Ishani Desai

item.title: Mexico to File Criminal Complaints in U.S. Over Immigrant Deaths
item.description: The nation’s president vowed to go “beyond diplomatic notes” after a U.S. immigration agent fatally shot Lorenzo Salgado Araujo, a Mexican man, in Texas this week.
item.dc:creator: Zane Irwin
item.media:credit: Meridith Kohut for The New York Times
item.media:description: A candlelight vigil on Wednesday in Houston, at the spot where Lorenzo Salgado Araujo was shot and killed a day earlier.

item.title: Royal Rapprochement: Harry and Meghan Pay a Rare Visit to the King and Queen
item.description: Friday’s visit to King Charles and Queen Camilla signaled a détente in relations with Prince Harry and his wife, who stepped down from royal duties and moved to the United States in 2020.
item.dc:creator: Zane Irwin and Megan Specia
item.category: Charles III, King of the United Kingdom
Camilla, Queen Consort of the United Kingdom
item.media:description: Prince Harry visiting a hospital in Birmingham, England, on Thursday.

item.title: Trump Cuts Habitat Protections for Endangered Species
item.description: The rule change ends a safeguard that had been in place for 50 years and could hasten the demise of imperiled animals.
item.dc:creator: Catrin Einhorn and Maxine Joselow
item.media:credit: Dave Sanders for The New York Times
item.media:description: Piping plovers need undisturbed beaches to nest and raise their young.

item.title: Sexual Assault and Killing of Girl Set Off Days of Protest in West Bengal
item.description: After promising to improve safety for women in the Indian state, the party of Prime Minister Narendra Modi is facing public anger after the death of an 11-year-old girl.
item.dc:creator: Suhasini Raj and Jeremy W. Peters
item.media:description: Protesting the sexual assault and killing of a young girl in Baruipur, India.

item.title: Museum Spreads 800 Pounds of Peanut Butter in Tribute to Dutch Artist
item.description: An installation honoring the artist, Wim T. Schippers, features a 270-square-foot hexagon spread on the floor of a museum in Rotterdam.
item.dc:creator: Natan Odenheimer
item.media:description: Workers at the Museum Boijmans Van Beuningen in Rotterdam, the Netherlands, recreated “Peanut Butter Floor,” an art installation by Wim T. Schippers, last week.

item.title: Ghanaian Influencer Is Extradited Over Romance Scam Targeting Older Americans
item.description: Prosecutors accused Frederick Kumi of running a scheme that defrauded Americans out of $8 million. He was flown to the United States on Thursday, his lawyer said.
item.dc:creator: Isabella Kwai

item.title: Man Arrested on Suspicion of Murdering Ann Widdecombe, Former U.K. Politician
item.description: The police said they had arrested a 26-year-old British man after Ann Widdecombe, a 78-year-old former lawmaker, was found dead with serious injuries on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Castle
item.media:description: Ann Widdecombe speaking at a council meeting in Southend-on-Sea, England, in 2022.

item.title: Trump Says Negotiations With Iran Will Continue as U.S. Ramps Up Strikes
item.description: U.S. forces hit more than 170 targets, including air defense systems, drone and missile storage sites, and military speedboats.
item.dc:creator: Eric Schmitt
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi/Polaris for The New York Times
item.media:description: The funeral procession in Tehran on Thursday for Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, Iran’s former leader, who was killed in February during U.S. and Israeli military strikes on his country.

item.title: Passenger Is Partly Sucked Through Window on Ryanair Subsidiary Flight, Reports Say
item.description: A 61-year-old man was being treated for friction burns after a window was damaged during a flight from Greece to Germany. The flight was operated by the Ryanair subsidiary Malta Air.
item.dc:creator: Victor Mather, Niki Kitsantonis and Niraj Chokshi
item.media:description: A Ryanair plane at Thessaloniki Airport in Greece.

item.title: Police Investigating Donations to Reform U.K., Reports Say
item.description: The police in London said they had questioned two people as part of an investigation into donations to a political party.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Castle
item.media:description: Nigel Farage, leader of Reform U.K., during a rally days before the 2024 British general election. He announced his candidacy only weeks earlier.

item.title: Greece Arrests 3 in Connection With Bombings Targeting Governing Party
item.description: The attacks killed one person and injured others. The Greek authorities said an investigation would continue.
item.dc:creator: Niki Kitsantonis
item.media:description: Damaged cars and a building in Thessaloniki, Greece, this month after attacks on residential buildings linked to the country’s governing party.

item.title: Mediators Trying to Pull U.S. and Iran Back From Brink, Officials Say
item.description: Qatari mediators were in Iran seeking to salvage the tattered truce. President Trump said talks with Tehran would continue, even if the cease-fire was “over.”
item.dc:creator: Adam Rasgon and Euan Ward
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Vessels in the Strait of Hormuz, as seen from Musandam, Oman, on Thursday.

item.title: Moscow Still Has Art and Culture. Just Don’t Say ‘Ukraine.’
item.description: An eerie limbo prevails at small art shows, independent theaters and private political clubs, where the war is the elephant in the room.
item.dc:creator: Ivan Nechepurenko and Nanna Heitmann
item.media:description: An art exhibition on a Moscow rooftop, in May.

item.title: After Venezuela Earthquakes, a Youth Baseball League Counts the Dead
item.description: Children who played for Venezuela’s youth baseball league, a symbol of national pride, have been left injured, orphaned or dead.
item.dc:creator: Luis Ferré-Sadurní, Isayen Herrera and Fabiola Ferrero

item.title: Can Hungarian Holocaust Survivors Find Justice in the U.S.?
item.description: For more than a decade, a group of survivors have fought a legal battle to get American courts to hear their suit against Hungary’s national railroad over its role in the atrocities.
item.dc:creator: Ephrat Livni
item.media:description: The Wall of Remembrance during a memorial service at the Holocaust Memorial Center in Budapest last year.

item.title: Fighting Fires With Figures, These Experts Are Trying to Stop Europe From Burning
item.description: A team of engineers, foresters and scientists is helping the continent prepare for wildfires from a giant science park in Italy. Their arsenal includes satellites, weather models and expert analysis.
item.dc:creator: Jeanna Smialek
item.media:description: Firefighters in Patras, Greece, in August. National authorities fight fires within their borders, but the European Union helps coordinate responses and sends additional resources to places in need.

item.title: Inside a Theater Sheltering Families in Lebanon
item.description: Our reporter Christina Goldbaum visits the Lebanese National Theater in Beirut to meet displaced families who have been sheltering there during the war.
item.dc:creator: Christina Goldbaum and Sutton Raphael

item.title: Iran’s Supreme Leader Remains Absent, a Void at the Top of the Regime
item.description: Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei did not appear at his father’s funeral this week, fueling speculation about his physical condition and leaving a power vacuum in a divided country.
item.dc:creator: Yeganeh Torbati
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi/Polaris for The New York Times
item.media:description: A pro-government demonstration in Tehran in May, with a banner showing Iran’s supreme leader, Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei.

item.title: China Launches Reusable Rocket, Clearing a Key Hurdle in Race With SpaceX
item.description: The launch and recovery of the Long March 10B could represent a long-awaited breakthrough for Chinese satellite companies.
item.dc:creator: Selam Gebrekidan and Pei-Lin Wu
item.media:credit: Visual China Group, via Getty Images
item.media:description: A Long March 10B rocket blasting off from the Wenchang spaceport in Hainan, China, on Friday.

item.title: Indian Diaspora, Now the Largest Foreign-Born Group in Australia, Welcomes Modi
item.description: A large, festive reception for the Indian prime minister in Melbourne belied tensions over migration in the country.
item.dc:creator: Victoria Kim
item.media:description: People cheering during the Melbourne Meets Modi community event at a stadium in Melbourne, Australia, on Friday.
